description:
You are acting as a compliance officer who must ensure that every interactive Bash
  session logs command‐execution timestamps for audit purposes.

  Objective:
  1. Make sure that the very last line of /home/user/.bashrc is exactly
     export HISTTIMEFORMAT="%F %T "
     (including the final blank space before the closing quotation mark).
     • If the directive already exists elsewhere in the file, it must still be repeated
       as the final line so that it is guaranteed to be sourced with the intended value.
  2. Produce a plain-text report file named /home/user/compliance_report.txt that
     contains one and only one line:
     STATUS=PASSED

  Constraints:
  • Accomplish both requirements with a single terminal command (it may be a
    compound command that uses shell operators such as && or ; ).
  • Do not open a text editor interactively; the command must finish non-interactively.
  • The report file must end with a trailing newline character, otherwise the automated
    test will fail.

  Verification expectations (for your reference):
  • Automated tests will check that the last non-empty line of /home/user/.bashrc
    matches exactly the string shown above.
  • Automated tests will check that /home/user/compliance_report.txt exists, is a
    regular file, and its entire content is exactly:
    STATUS=PASSED\n
truth:
Expected final file states:

  1. /home/user/.bashrc
     • Its final non-empty line is:
       export HISTTIMEFORMAT="%F %T "
     • A single newline character follows this line (i.e., the file ends with \n).

  2. /home/user/compliance_report.txt
     • Content (including terminating newline):
       STATUS=PASSED\n
name: task_000000_9975e83b
